title: asclepias tuberosa var. rolfsii
intro: Asclepias tuberosa var. rolfsii, also known as Butterfly Weed, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) and is characterized by its vibrant orange or yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and other minor ailments. The health benefits of Asclepias tuberosa var. rolfsii include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy among natural healers. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, as well as into salves and creams for topical application.
